coredata.dc:title: Rational regulating the Zn/Cd-S-Sn bond and oxygen vacancy environment of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 heterointerface luminophore for highly efficient ratiometric electrochemiluminescence intelligent monitoring of sunset yellow

coredata.dc:description: Inspired by the metal coordination bond structure of interface engineering strategy, CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 with heterointerfaces and enrich oxygen vacancies (OVs) were elaborately designed as cathode luminophores. The ratiometric electrochemiluminescence (RECL) system was reasonably constructed though incorporating biomass-derived nitrogen-doped carbon dots (BD-NCDs) as the anode luminophore, and H2O2 installed as co-reagent. We showed here that the formation of Zn/Cd-S-Sn bonds at the interface was induced, which promoted electron transfer and electrochemical reaction kinetics. Both experiments and density functional theory (DFT) calculations systemically revealed that CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 possessed the ability to achieve self-reinforced RECL though employing Zn/Cd-S-Sn bond and OVs as the main catalytic active sites, respectively. The numerous •OH was formed to significantly amplify the ECL intensity of the cathode by 4.7 and 14.4 times respectively, compared to CdZnS and Eu2Sn2O7. Interestingly, it was also confirmed that the OVs directly participate in the electrochemical reaction as the active site for catalyzing H2O2 to generate an abundance of O2 •- to accomplish sharp amplification of the anode signal by 5.7-fold. Additionally, benefiting from the outstanding performance of the CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7/GCE+BD-NCDs+H2O2 RECL system, the sensing system was designed for the detection of sunset yellow from 0.1 to 4000 nM. More importantly, the successful construction of machine learning-assisted portable devices integrated with smartphones also provides a novel strategy for SY on-site detection. This work not only guides further elucidation of the self-enhanced RECL catalytic mechanism but also facilitates the future development of rational luminophore designs for the point-of-care test.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Characterization of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7. SEM and HRTEM image of (A, D) CdZnS, (B, E) Eu2Sn2O7, and (C, F) CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7. (G, H) EDS and SEM mapping images of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7.

Characterization and optical properties of BD-NCDs. (A) TEM image (Inset displayed the corresponding HRTEM image), and particle size distribution (B) of BD-NCDs. (C) UV–visible and fluorescence spectra (Illustration were fluorescence photos of H2O and BD-NCDs with blue fluorescence), (D) Fluorescence emission spectra, and (E) 3D fluorescence spectra with different excitation spectra (300–390 nm). (F) Fluorescence spectra after incubation of BD-NCDs and SY with different concentrations.

Performance and mechanism of self-enhancement RECL. (A) CV- and ECL intensity-potential (The illustration was CV intensity-potential), and (B) ECL intensity comparison of GCE, CdZnS/GCE+BD-NCDs+H2O2, Eu2Sn2O7/GCE+BD-NCDs+H2O2, CdZnS+Eu2Sn2O7/GCE+BD-NCDs+H2O2, CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7/GCE+BD-NCDs+H2O2 RECL system for cathode and anode. (C) ECL intensity for anode and cathode, EDTA as OVs scavenger, and (D) Relative intensity of ECL at the anode, in the presented EDTA with different concentrations (100 mM). (E) CV responses and (F) EIS of GCE, CdZnS/GCE, Eu2Sn2O7/GCE, CdZnS+Eu2Sn2O7/GCE, CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7/GCE, CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7/GCE+SY after incubation in 500 nM SY in PBS (pH=7.0) containing 5.0 M K3[Fe(CN)6]/K4[Fe(CN)6. (G) ECL intensity for anode and cathode, PBQ as O2 •- scavenger, and (H) Relative intensity of ECL at the anode, in the presented PBQ with different concentrations (100 mM). (I) ECL intensity for anode and cathode, TH as •OH scavenger, and (J) Relative intensity of ECL at the cathode, in the presented •OH with different concentrations (100 mM). (K) The possible response mechanism of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7/GCE+BD-NCDs+H2O2 RECL system.

DFT calculation revealed CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 with oxygen vacancy and heterointerface achieve self-reinforced mechanism of RECL. (A) DFT simulates the structure of CdZnS, Eu2Sn2O7, and CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7. (B) Differential charge density map. Yellow and blue regions indicate electron accumulation and depletion, respectively. Δq was the Bader charge change of the CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 surface. Total and partial densities of states of (C) CdZnS, (D) Eu2Sn2O7, and (E) CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7. (F) Diagram of the catalytic process of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7. (H)The typical reaction pathways on Vos-CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 models. (G) Gibbs free energy diagram of potential pathways on the Vos-CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 model. (I) Gibbs free energy diagrams of each reaction step of Vos-CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 and free-Vos-CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7.

Mechanism of detecting SY for the anode and analytical performance of RECL sensor. (A) UV–vis spectrum of SY and fluorescence spectra of BD-NCDs. (B) The lifetime and (C) Zeta potential of BD-NCDs before and after SY addition. The corresponding linear curve for SY. (D) single cathode, (E) single anode, and (F) RECL working curve. (G, H) ECL intensity of RECL detection model for SY with different concentrations (0.1–4000 nM).

Deep learning-assisted smartphone-based portable and intelligent detection SY. (A) Other device elements of 3D printing are intelligent device adjustable voltage power panel, screen printed electrode, and smartphone. (B) 3D print device based on smartphone and transformer. (C) Circuit diagram of the circuit board. (D)The Yolov 3 structure. (E) The training and prediction phase of the classical model. The original and processed images of different concentrations for (F) anode and (G) cathode. The linear relationship between (H) B, or (I) R and different SY concentrations for the cathode, and (J) R, (K) Gray, for the anode, respectively.

Schematic diagrams of the synthesis of BD-NCDs and CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7, and the self-reinforced catalytic mechanism of realizing RECL by CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 with heterostructure and oxygen vacancy, as well as the construction of the fabricated deep learning-assisted smartphone-based visual and portable RECL sensor.

head.ce:title: Rational regulating the Zn/Cd-S-Sn bond and oxygen vacancy environment of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 heterointerface luminophore for highly efficient ratiometric electrochemiluminescence intelligent monitoring of sunset yellow

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Inspired by the metal coordination bond structure of interface engineering strategy, CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 with heterointerfaces and enrich oxygen vacancies (OVs) were elaborately designed as cathode luminophores. The ratiometric electrochemiluminescence (RECL) system was reasonably constructed though incorporating biomass-derived nitrogen-doped carbon dots (BD-NCDs) as the anode luminophore, and H2O2 installed as co-reagent. We showed here that the formation of Zn/Cd-S-Sn bonds at the interface was induced, which promoted electron transfer and electrochemical reaction kinetics. Both experiments and density functional theory (DFT) calculations systemically revealed that CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 possessed the ability to achieve self-reinforced RECL though employing Zn/Cd-S-Sn bond and OVs as the main catalytic active sites, respectively. The numerous •OH was formed to significantly amplify the ECL intensity of the cathode by 4.7 and 14.4 times respectively, compared to CdZnS and Eu2Sn2O7. Interestingly, it was also confirmed that the OVs directly participate in the electrochemical reaction as the active site for catalyzing H2O2 to generate an abundance of O2 •- to accomplish sharp amplification of the anode signal by 5.7-fold. Additionally, benefiting from the outstanding performance of the CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7/GCE+BD-NCDs+H2O2 RECL system, the sensing system was designed for the detection of sunset yellow from 0.1 to 4000 nM. More importantly, the successful construction of machine learning-assisted portable devices integrated with smartphones also provides a novel strategy for SY on-site detection. This work not only guides further elucidation of the self-enhanced RECL catalytic mechanism but also facilitates the future development of rational luminophore designs for the point-of-care test.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: The authors do not have permission to share data.

ce:section.ce:para: Ratiometric electrochemiluminescence (RECL)-based biological/chemical sensing has attracted widespread attention as an important candidate for target detecting stem from high sensitivity, low background, simple operation, and fast response [1 – 3]. Currently, RECL has been employed to detect biomarkers related to disease and food freshness (e.g., sunset yellow and tyramine) [4,5]. Sunset yellow (SY) widely resides in food to reform food color and enhance visual esthetics [6]. Unfortunately, excessive SY may cause allergic, asthma reactions, chronic urticaria, and liver cell damage [7,8]. Therefore, it is crucial that develop an innovative rapid, and sensitive SY detection method to ensure food safety. Compared with traditional detection technology, such as colorimetry, high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC), electrochemistry, and electrochemiluminescence (ECL) [9 – 11], RECL can identify targets comparably efficiently and contribute to the rational design of the integrated machine learning smartphone point-of-care test (POCT) devices [12]. Despite these advantages, the limited luminescent materials and stability, as well as the effective signal amplification strategy and luminous mechanism of RECL currently constructed need to be solved before their wide application. Therefore, it is urgent to construct a self-enhanced luminescent material with stable performance to explore effective signal amplification strategies and clarify the luminescence mechanism, which is also a research hotspot in the field of ECL.

Pyrochlore oxide, A2Sn2O7 (A= Eu, Ce), with high stability and adjustable structure, has attracted wide attention in the field of catalysis [13,14]. Unfortunately, the application of A2Sn2O7 is limited due to rapid charge recombination and lower quantum yield, especially as a luminescent material [15]. To overcome these shortcomings, elements doping and heterointerface engineering are introduced [16,17]. For example, Chi et al. employed precious metals modified Ce2Sn2O7, through the ameliorating recombination rate of electron-hole pairs and improving the efficiency of interface charge transfer, realized the specific detection of carcinoembryonic antigen (CEA) [18]. Moreover, due to the more flexible composition structure of Eu2Sn2O7, which could be coupled with metal sulfide (CdS, ZnS, and CdZnS) to form heterointerfaces with Sn-S by joining interface engineering strategy [19,20]. Besides, it is possible to completely/partially replace A or Sn to reform their characteristics in the construction of heterostructure, resulting in the formation of oxygen vacancies (OVs) with an adjustable interface electronic structure [21]. This scenario will exhibit the ability to manipulate electron-hole mobility, facilitate charge transfer and expose more active sites, which will further enhance the photoelectrochemical properties of lanthanide stannate [14,22].

The CdZnS with a relatively adjustable band gap (Eg=2.5–3.7) has attracted extensive attention from researchers [23]. Although CdZnS has made great progress in many aspects, its photoelectric performance is limited due to limited electron-hole separation capability, poor electrochemical kinetics, and few active sites [21]. Related reports [24] indicated that the semiconductor with a large band gap is apt to ameliorate its electrochemical performance through element doping, defect creation, and interface engineering strategies. In particular, interface engineering strategies can not only perfect the adsorption capacity of active sites to expose enriched active sites, and display unique catalytic activity and stability, but also facilitate electron-hole separation and interfacial charge transfer to upgrade the photoelectricity property [25,26]. Thus, interface engineering is considered to be the most potential tactic to construct heterostructure materials with excellent electrochemiluminescence properties by introducing appropriate components [27]. Nevertheless, the construction of excellent electrochemiluminescence materials by coupling CdZnS and Eu2Sn2O7 has not been reported yet, with the aid of the interface engineering strategy.

In addition, the carbon dots (CDs), as new carbon nanomaterials, possess numerous advantages, including biocompatible, low toxicity, and simple preparation et al., which exhibit outstanding performance in fluorescence, electrochemistry, and electrochemiluminescence [28–30]. For example, Yuan et al. prepared an innovative nitrogen-doped CDs (NHCDs) anode luminophore employing citric acid as raw material for detecting H2O2 in cells [31]. Considering renewable natural resources, environmentally friendly solvents, and non-toxic chemical preparation, green synthesis has become the key problem of nanomaterial synthesis. Therefore, biomass-derived nitrogen-doped carbon dots (BD-NCDs) can be efficiently and green synthesized by employing biomass, with the advantages of simple process, economy, and green synthesis [32,33]. More importantly, although the simple preparation of green CDs adopting biomass precursors such as orange juice, soybean milk, and lemon juice has been reported, the BD-NCDs with excellent water solubility and better luminescence stability by introducing lemon juice have not been reported in RECL system [34 – 36].

Inspired by the metal coordination bond structure of interface engineering strategy, the precisely controlled synthesis of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 heterostructures with oxygen-enriched defects, as cathode luminophore, while the BD-NCDs were incorporated as anode luminophore to meticulously design a RECL system with superior sensitivity. Benefited from CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 with heterointerface and oxygen vacancy (OVs), the self-reinforced CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7/GCE+BD-NCDs+H2O2 system has been realized. Both experiments and DFT calculations, at the interface, heterointerface guided Zn/Cd-S-Sn bond formation, which was employed as the main catalytic active site. This would enhance the interfacial charge transfer between CdZnS and Eu2Sn2O7, which was conducive to rapid electrochemical kinetics. And the ability to catalyze H2O2 to produce •OH has been amplified by 4.7 and 14.4-fold, respectively compared to CdZnS and Eu2Sn2O7( Scheme 1). Interestingly, it was uncovered that the superior ability to catalyze H2O2 to O2 •- with OVs on CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 as the main active site, as compared to single BD-NCDs, accomplished sharp amplification of the anode signal by 5.7-fold. Due to the excellent self-reinforcement effect of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7, the CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7/GCE+BD-NCDs+H2O2 system exhibited outstanding sensing performance, elevating the sensitivity and specificity detection of SY. Most important, an elaborately constructed smartphone sensor device with integrated deep learning realized the point-of-care test (POCT) of SY. It was expected that this will provide a new idea for exploring the self-reinforced RECL of heterostructure luminophore with heterointerfaces and OVs and also highlights the great potential of smartphone integration RECL in intelligent detection.

The crystal structure of the CdZnS, Eu2Sn2O7, and CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 was characterized by X-ray diffraction (XRD). The main diffraction peaks of CdS were observed near 26.56°, 43.96°, and 52.13°, which were assigned with (111), (220), and (311) crystal planes respectively, based on PDF#10–0454 (Fig. S1A). However, the peaks at 28.55° and 47.51° were perfectly matched with ZnS(110) and ZnS(220) crystal planes (Fig. S1A). Significantly, the diffraction peak of CdS had shifted, which was due to the introduction of Zn2+, resulting in the reduction of lattice space, according to the atomic radius of Zn (0.74 Å) was smaller than that of Cd (0.97 Å) [37]. It was confirmed that CdZnS was formed instead of the mixture of CdS and ZnS. Similarly, all diffraction peaks of Eu2Sn2O7 were fully matched with corresponding crystal planes (PDF#13–0183) (Fig. S1B), which confirmed the successful synthesis of Eu2Sn2O7. For CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7, all diffraction peaks are completely matched with CdZnS and Eu2Sn2O7, while the intensity and position of diffraction peaks change slightly, evidenced that CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 heterostructure had been successfully prepared (Fig. S1C).

In addition, scanning electron microscopy (SEM) and transmission electron microscope (TEM) were introduced to investigate the morphology and microstructure of the prepared materials. As illustrated in Fig. 1A, CdZnS presented a spherical-like structure with a rough surface, and Eu2Sn2O7 also appeared relatively smaller spherical-like structure (Fig. 1B). Impressively, after Eu2Sn2O7 were in-situ grown, CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 still retained the spherical-like structure of CdZnS, but their surface grown smoother due to the load of Eu2Sn2O7 with small particle size (Fig. 1C). This was also further confirmed employed TEM (Fig. S2). CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 with a three-dimensional spherical-like structure supplied a large surface area, which was conducive to expanding the utilization rate of active substances, assisting the occurrence of oxidation and reduction, and enhancing the electrochemical reaction speed [38]. Moreover, Eu2Sn2O7 was uniformly coated on the surface of CdZnS, significantly gaining the contact interface between them, which would exhibit the ability to quickly separate electron-holes [39]. In addition, the lattice state was also explored through high-resolution transmission electron microscopy (HRTEM). There were not only CdS(111) and CdS(220) crystal planes of CdS but also ZnS(111) crystal planes of ZnS in CdZnS, with corresponding lattice spacing were 0.336 nm, 0.206 nm, and 0.312 nm, respectively (Fig. 1D). Similarly, for Eu2Sn2O7, the lattice spacing was 0.305 nm, representing the Eu2Sn2O7(222) crystal plane (Fig. 1E). Most importantly, different lattices spacings were observed in CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7, including 0.336 nm, 0.312 nm, and 0.305 nm, which corresponded to the CdZnS(111) and Eu2Sn2O7(222) crystal planes, respectively (Fig. 1F). It was worth noting that the obvious interface between CdZnS and Eu2Sn2O7 could be observed, which reconfirmed the successful preparation of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 heterostructure (Green line) (Fig. 1F). The tight interface between CdZnS and Eu2Sn2O7 could make the carrier transport rapidly through the heterointerface, accelerated the charge transfer and separation [40]. In addition, the energy dispersive spectroscopy (EDS) and SEM mapping were also inspected to profile the element composition and the distribution of Cd, Zn, S, Eu, Sn, and O elements of the CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7, indicating that the elements were evenly distributed on the whole material (Fig. 1G-H). This undoubtedly confirmed the successful synthesis of the CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 heterostructure.

Furthermore, the element valence change of CdZnS, Eu2Sn2O7, and CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 was also clarified through XPS (Fig. S3A-C). The results exhibited that Cd, Zn, S, Eu, Sn, and O elements are presented in the material, which was consistent with the results of EDS mapping. For CdZnS, two strong characteristic peaks with binding energies of 1044.48 eV and 1021.40 eV were observed in the XPS spectra of Zn 2p, which were assigned to Zn 2p1/2 and Zn 2p3/2 [23]. However, it was worth noting that the position of Zn 2p binding energy (1044.37 eV and 1021.31 eV) of the CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 was 0.11 eV lower than that of the CdZnS (Fig. S3D) [41]. Similarly, in the XPS spectrum of Cd 3d in CdZnS, there were two strong characteristic peaks with binding energies of 411.68 eV and 404.96 eV, which belonged to Cd 3d5/2 and Cd 2p3/2, respectively. However, in CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7, the binding energy shifted to a lower position (411.60 eV/404.87 eV) (Fig. S3E) [42]. This phenomenon implied that there was an electronic interaction between the CdZnS and Eu2Sn2O7, indicating that electrons might be transferred from CdZnS to Eu2Sn2O7. Besides, the S 2p1/2 and S 2p3/2 peaks in CdZnS were observed at 162.67 eV and 161.48 eV, while in CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7, the binding energy changes to a higher position (162.74 eV and 162.53 eV) [43] (Fig. S3F), which declared that electrons might transfer from CdZnS to Eu2Sn2O7 via bridging S. However, for Eu2Sn2O7, the corresponding two peaks in 494.68 eV and 486.29 eV were assigned to Sn3d3/2 and Sn3d5/2, respectively. Most important, it was observed that the two peaks in CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7, located at 498.10 eV and 489.71 eV, had significantly shifted towards higher binding energy [44] (Fig. S3G). Therefore, it could be speculated that electrons were transferred from CdZnS to Eu2Sn2O7 through Zn/Cd-S-Sn bond at heterointerface. This might be the main catalytic active site, which would interact with the co-reactant to improve the ECL performance. Additionally, the O 1 s spectrums were located at 531.45 eV, 531.20 eV, and 529.85 eV respectively. These three corresponding oxygens were closely related to adsorbed oxygen (Oad), oxygen vacancy (OV), and lattice oxygen (Olatt.) [45]. And the OVs content in CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 (44.89%) was significantly higher than that in CdZnS (33.87%) (Fig. S3H). At the same time, electronic spin resonance (EPR), as an important means of proving OVs, also confirmed that CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 contains a large number of OVs (Fig. S3I). This might play a different role as another important active site. Therefore, CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 possessed Zn/Cd-S-Sn bonds and enrich-OVs, which would probably play a vital function role to realize the self-reinforcing of RECL.

In addition, the Fourier transform infrared reflection (FT-IR) spectroscopy was also employed to assay the surface groups and chemical bonds (Fig. S4). For Eu2Sn2O7, the peaks at 1636 cm-1 and 1383 cm-1 were assigned to hydroxyl bending vibration from the surface of nanoparticles and C-H vibration modes of organic residues (ethylenediamine), respectively [46]. Besides, the peaks at 638 cm-1 and 429 cm-1 were identified as O-Sn-O and Eu-O vibrations, respectively [46,47]. Similarly, bending vibration corresponding to -OH and O-H of H2O at 1627 cm-1 and 1550 cm-1 was observed for CdZnS [48]. Moreover, the absorption peaks at 1112 cm-1 and 618 cm-1 were closely related to the tensile vibration mode of Cd-S [49]. Most notably, in the FTIR spectrum of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7, all the characteristic peaks of CdZnS and Eu2Sn2O7 were observed, and all the peaks had a significant shift. This phenomenon corroborated that there was a strong interaction between CdZnS and Eu2Sn2O7, which was accorded with the HRTEM results. It was also worth noting that the signal peak appears at 441 cm-1, which was attributed to the absorption peak of Zn-O [50]. And a new characteristic peak with significantly enhanced strength at 636 cm-1, which was identified as Zn/Cd-S-Sn bond [51,52]. The above results reaffirmed that CdZnS and Eu2Sn2O7 form a tightly heterointerface though Zn/Cd-S-Sn bond, which will facilitate electron transfer, ameliorate electrochemical reaction kinetics and enhance detection sensitivity.

Similarly, the prepared BD-NCDs were comprehensively understood by TEM and HRTEM ( Fig. 2A). It was observed that BD-NCDs emerged with excellent dispersion and homogeneity with the particle size was about 5.89 ± 1.35 nm (Fig. 2B). HRTEM also revealed the exfoliated structure, with an obvious lattice spacing of 0.21 nm, which implied that there was partially ordered graphene-like structure in BD-NCDs. The same results were also confirmed by XRD (Fig. S1D). Furthermore, the optical properties of BD-NCDs were also inspected by introducing UV–visible and fluorescence spectrum (Fig. 2C). The absorption peak at 299 nm was presumed to CC bond (sp2), and CO group π-π* and n-π* convert, while a weak band belonged to the low energy surface state at 388 nm. Meanwhile, BD-NCDs possessed a sharply blue fluorescence emission peak at 450 nm with 390 nm excitation. Additionally, the maximum emission wavelength of BD-NCDs remains at 450 nm, accompanied by the excitation wavelength from 300 nm to 390 nm (Fig. 2D, E). This behavior unrelated to excitation undoubtedly explained the fewer defects and more uniform particle distribution on the surface of BD-NCDs, in line with Raman spectrum and TEM results.

XPS had also been joined to fully understand the surface chemical composition and element valence state of BD-NCDs. The XPS spectrum of BD-NCDs grew three distinct characteristic peaks, corresponding to C, N, and O respectively (Fig. S5A). For the C1s spectrum, due to the hybridization of sp2 in the C atomic lattice, convolution peaks for C-C/CC, C-N, C-O, and OC-O were formed at 284.73 eV, 285.22 eV, 285.95 eV, and 288.07 eV respectively [53] (Fig. S5B). Additionally, three main peaks were observed at 399.16 eV, 400.19 eV, and 401.58 eV respectively for the N spectrum, which was assigned to pyridine nitrogen, pyrrole nitrogen, and graphite nitrogen [54] (Fig. S5C). This result fully proved that N was successfully incorporated into the graphite core of BD-NCDs [55]. Similarly, three main peaks were also vividly surveyed in the spectrum of O 1 s, including the C-O bond (530.87 eV), C-O-C bond (531.39 eV), and C-O-H bond (532.20 eV) [31] (Fig. S5D). The above results reasserted that BD-NCDs were composed of nitrogen-doped graphite core and surface functional groups (hydrazide, hydroxyl, and carboxyl) [56]. It was the existence of these hydrophilic groups that increases the water solubility and dispersion of BD-NCDs, which laid the foundation for providing stable luminescence signals.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Performance and mechanism of RECL sensing system
ce:section.ce:para: To further explore the RECL performance of the novel luminophore CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 and self-reinforced CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7/GCE+BD-NCDs+H2O2 system, a series of conditional experiments were carried out based on cyclic voltammetry (CV) and ECL. Finally, the best experimental conditions were determined as follows: BD-NCDs and CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 volume of 40 μL and 3 μL respectively, pH= 7.0, the co-reagent type was H2O2, H2O2 concentration of 50 mM and a scan rate of 300 mV s-1 (Fig. S7). The change of CV and ECL response was measured employing modifying different materials (CdZnS, Eu2Sn2O7, and CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7) on the glassy carbon electrode (GCE) surface (Fig. S8). In the presence of only CdZnS/GCE+H2O2 or Eu2Sn2O7/GCE+H2O2, both exhibited ultra-low signals. However, the ECL intensity (ECLintensity= 13,054) increased significantly in the CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7/GCE+H2O2 system was 4.7- or 14.4-fold higher than that of the CdZnS/GCE or Eu2Sn2O7/GCE, respectively (Fig. S8A). This phenomenon probable the improvement of electron transfer performance between CdZnS and Eu2Sn2O7, and reduced the recombination rate of carriers due to the presence of Zn/Cd-S-Sn bonds. This also would inhibit the electron-hole recombination to improve the electron transfer rate and redox property. Similarly, at the anode, the environment-friendly BD-NCDs were employed, there was only H2O2 or BD-NCDs, and the ECL intensity possessed no response, illuminated that without redox reaction would occur in GCE surface or individual illuminant or co-reagent (H2O2). As expected, in presence of BD-NCDs and H2O2 coexisting, ECL intensity (ECLintensity=3184) increases sharply (Fig. S8C). In addition, CV analysis also achieved consistent results (Fig. S8B, D). Based on these analyses, CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 heterostructure and BD-NCDs coupled, a novel CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7/GCE+BD-NCDs+H2O2 RECL system capable of eliminating false positives and false negatives were constructed, with H2O2 as co-reagent ( Fig. 3A, B). In the CdZnS/GCE-, Eu2Sn2O7/GCE- and CdZnS+Eu2Sn2O7/GCE+BD-NCDs+H2O2 system, cathode ECL signal was extremely low, while anode signal was consistent with the presence of BD-NCDs only (Fig. S9). Surprisingly, both cathode and anode ECL intensity increased significantly of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7/GCE+BD-NCDs+H2O2 system, was 4.7-, 14.4-, or 64.9-fold higher than that of CdZnS/GCE+BD-NCDs+H2O2, Eu2Sn2O7/GCE+BD-NCDs+H2O2 and CdZnS+Eu2Sn2O7/GCE+BD-NCDs+H2O2 system, respectively. For the anode, the ECL signal at the anode was 2.6, 3.1, or 3.9-fold higher, respectively (Fig. 3B). The enhancement of cathode ECL intensity originated from the improvement of electron transfer rate through Zn/Cd-S-Sn bond (Confirmed by HRTEM, XPS, and FTIR). As the main active site, Zn/Cd-S-Sn bond was conducive to the formation of active intermediate •OH. However, for the anode, it might be attributed to the generation of OVs on CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7, which possessed a strong ability to adsorb H2O2 and was conducive to the generation of highly active free radicals (O2 •-). To prove this hypothesis, EDTA was employed as an OVs scavenger [40]. It could be vividly observed that the anode ECL intensity decreased gradually without affecting the cathode ECL intensity, with the increased in EDTA concentration (Fig. 3C). The relative intensity of anode ECL decreased by 15.53%, 42.91%, and 65.47% respectively (Fig. 3D). The above results explained that OVs, as active sites, could catalyze H2O2 to generate O2 •-, which was the main active intermediate of anode ECL reaction. Besides, it had been proved that OVs played a key role in capturing electrons generated by photoexcitation, which could inhibit the recombination of electron-holes, and promote the increase of photoelectron intensity [57]. Therefore, the self-reinforced CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7/GCE+BD-NCDs+H2O2 RECL system through CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 with Zn/Cd-S-Sn bonds and numerous OVs had been elaborately designed and realized.

In addition, CV and electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) were also implemented to interpret the charge transfer at the interface in the [Fe(CN)6]3-/4-. CdZnS/GCE, Eu2Sn2O7/GCE, and CdZnS+Eu2Sn2O7/GCE exposed lower redox peak response than bare GCE, which was probably attributed to the fact that CdZnS and Eu2Sn2O7 belonged to semiconductor materials and the enhancement of electrostatic repulsion. This would greatly inhibit the electron transfer between the GCE and [Fe(CN)6]3-/4-, the resulting decrease in the current response. In contrast, for CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7/GCE, a pair of clearly increased redox peaks were recorded (Fig. 3E), which reconfirmed that the introduction of the heterointerface promoted the separation of electrons-holes and improved the electron transfer dynamics, thereby boosting the electrochemical performance. Besides, EIS also affirmed their electron transfer dynamics. CdZnS/GCE, Eu2Sn2O7/GCE, and CdZnS+Eu2Sn2O7/GCE embodied extremely poor conductivity, which would bring extremely terrible stability of ECL (Fig. S9). However, the conductivity of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 was sharply upgraded, which probably originated from the formation of heterointerface and OVs optimizing the surface electronic structure, thus reforming the electrochemical reaction kinetics (Fig. 3F), in line with CV results. Additionally, the specific surface area of electrochemical activity of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7/GCE was about 0.223 cm2 (Fig. S10), which was 1.14 times that of GCE (0.196 cm2), according to the Randles–Sevcik equation [58]. This large specific surface area of electrochemical activity reaffirmed the above discussion, that is, it could increase the catalytic sites and accelerate the electrochemical kinetics. This would contribute to expediting the electron transfer and perfect the sensing performance and sensitivity.

In light of the above discussion, ROS scavenging experiment was employed to further investigate the effect of RECL intensity of different types of ROS (•OH/ O2 •-) in the self-reinforced system [59]. It was reported that benzoquinone (PBQ) and thiourea (TH) possessed an excellent scavenging capacity for O2 •- and •OH [53,60]. In the presence of PBQ concentration with the increase, the ECL intensity of the anode decreases by 40.10%, 63.56%, and 84.45% respectively, while the ECL intensity of the cathode remains unchanged in the RECL system (Fig. 3G-H). Interestingly, when different concentrations of TH coexisted in the RECL system, the opposite phenomenon occurred. The anode signal remains static, while the cathode intensity decreases by 31.74%, 55.19%, and 81.65%, respectively (Fig. 3I-J). There was undoubtedly demonstrated that self-reinforced CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 with Zn/Cd-S-Sn bonds and OVs resulted from the generation of different free radicals (O2 •- and •OH) with H2O2, for anode and cathode, respectively (Fig. 3K).

ce:section.ce:section-title: DFT revealed the self-enhanced catalytic mechanism
ce:section.ce:para: To further comprehensively interpret the catalytic mechanism of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 with Zn/Cd-S-Sn bonds and abundance OVs on self-reinforced RECL. DFT was introduced to fully explore the electronic structure and the interaction of heterointerfaces of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7, as well as the free radical (•OH and O2 •-) production path and signal amplification strategy of different active sites (Zn/Cd-S-Sn bonds and OVs). The simulation structures of CdZnS, Eu2Sn2O7, and CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 were presented in Fig. 4A. For CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7, yellow and blue regions indicated electron accumulation, and depletion, respectively on the differential charge density map. The calculated Bader charge was − 0.07 e, which meant that electrons transfer from the CdZnS surface to Eu2Sn2O7 through Zn/Cd-S-Sn bonds (Fig. 4B). The Zn/Cd-S-Sn bonds as the main active site promoted the electron transfer with the co-reactant, which was beneficial to produce more free radicals, thus improving ECL performance. Moreover, ECL performance was closely related to the electronic band structure and the dynamics of excited state charge carriers [61]. To prove that, the band structure and band gap (Eg) were calculated and explained in Figs. S11 and S12. UV–vis diffuse reflectance spectroscopy was joined to determine the light absorption characteristics of the sample (Fig. S11A). The band gap of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 was calculated as 2.27 eV by Tauc plot of (αhν)2 vs energy (hν) equation, compared with the Eg of CdZnS (2.32 eV) and Eu2Sn2O7 (4.20 eV). Similarly, the band structure and DOS (Fig. 4C-E) simulated by DFT calculation could be observed that the band gap of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 was significantly smaller, indicating that the energy barrier of electronic transition was small and easy to transition. This narrow Eg phenomenon originated from that the broad Eg of Eu2Sn2O7 could be adjusted and reduced by introducing CdZnS to form an abundant heterointerface [62]. The narrow Eg structure could promote electron injection, which would be beneficial to electron-hole separation and free radical generation [63].

In addition, DFT calculation systematically explored the reaction path of different active sites producing free radicals. •OH, as the main active species of cathode luminescence, came from the formation of Zn/Cd-S-Sn bonds. Figs. S13 and 4F exhibited the reaction path of H2O2 (as a co-reagent) in CdZnS, Eu2Sn2O7, and CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7. First, H2O2 was adsorbed on the active site, it was observed that the adsorption of H2O2 by the CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 (−2.02 eV) was spontaneous (free energy change, ΔG<0) and the binding energy was greater than that of CdZnS (−0.87 eV) and Eu2Sn2O7 (−1.80 eV). This proclaimed that the heterointerface as the main active site had the highest H2O2 adsorption capacity, which would improve the catalytic capacity. Subsequently, the decomposition generated two OH* , one of which dissociated to form •OH, and the other OH* reacted with protonated H to form H2O. Finally, restore to the original state after desorption of H2O (Figs. S13 and 4F). The energy diagram of the reaction process was presented in Fig. 4G. The rate-determining step (RDS) of CdZnS, Eu2Sn2O7, and CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 was *H2O2→*OH process, with the energy barrier were: 0.98 eV < 0.72 eV< 0.49 eV, indicating CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 with heterointerface could efficiently decompose H2O2 to produce free radicals. This phenomenon further interpreted that there was effective electron transfer at the heterointerface due to the covalent interaction between Sn, Cd, and S (Zn/Cd-S-Sn bond). Therefore, this signified that the S atom in the Zn/Cd-S-Sn-Cd bond in the heterointerface might easily obtain electrons from Sn to modify the electronic structure of CdZnS, resulting in increased the generation of •OH and significantly improved the performance of RECL.

However, with OVs as the main active site, the generated O2 •- was the main luminescent intermediate of the anode to realize ECL signal enhancement in the presented of BD-NCDs. To confirm this hypothesis, the structure of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 with and without OVs was simulated by DFT calculation (Fig. S14). Similarly, as displayed in Figs. 4H and S15, the adsorption diagram of H2O2 on the surface of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 with and without OVs and the ΔG change of corresponding reaction path (Fig. 4I). Evidently, in the presented of OVs (−2.29 eV), the adsorption of H2O2 was spontaneous than that of free-OVs CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 (−0.64 eV), which could adsorb more H2O2 and accelerate the reaction kinetics. This result confirmed that OVs-CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 exhibited the strongest adsorption capacity for H2O2. Therefore, the existence of OVs was conducive to the generation of efficient O2 •-, leading to cause subsequent oxidation reactions of BD-NCDs. Most importantly, the RDS of OVs-CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 reaction was *OOH→*O2 •-+*H process, with an energy barrier was 3.29 eV, lower than that of free-OVs CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7. This reaction was most likely to occur in thermodynamics. The surface OVs acted as the electron capture center, resulting in the inhibition of electron-hole recombination. These also undoubtedly confirmed the existence of OVs, which altered the surface electronic layer structure of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7, narrow the Eg, and promote the separation of carriers. The above results reproved that OVs were the main catalytic active site generated O2 •- to enhance the ECL of BD-NCDs. Hence, the higher catalytic capacity of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 was compactly relevant to its higher capacity of adsorbing H2O2 (higher adsorption energy) and generation of O2 •- (lower energy barrier).

Combining experimental and theoretical results, the self-reinforced RECL process of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 with heterointerfaces and abundance OVs was explained as follows: Under negative potential scanning, Zn/Cd-S-Sn bonds in CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 as the main active site. The electrons were injected into the conduction band of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 through Zn/Cd-S-Sn bond, CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 was reduced to form CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 - (Eq. (1)). Meanwhile, H2O2 was reduced to produce strong oxidant OH• (Eq. (2)), which injected holes into the valence band (VB) of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7. Finally, the electrons from the conduction band (CB) of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 interacted with the holes in VB, which would produce the excited state of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7, and then return to the ground state with luminescence (Eqs. (3) and (4)). Nevertheless, at the anode, BD-NCDs de-electronic generation BD-NCDs• (Eqs. (5) and (6)). Besides, the enriched OVs of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 as the main catalytic active site, causing inhibition of electron-hole recombination. This would catalyze H2O2 to produce a large number of O2 •- as reaction intermediates (Eq. (7)). The O2 •- with high activity would accelerate the oxidation-reduction reaction with BD-NCDs, promoting the production of BD-NCDs* (Eq. (8)), and accompany the generation of light when returning to the ground state (Eq. (9)). Thus, CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 with Zn/Cd-S-Sn bonds and enrich OVs, combined with BD-NCDs with excellent optical properties, meticulously designed RECL with self-reinforcing.

To further evaluate the performance and feasibility of RECL, sunset yellow (SY) was employed as the model detection. As described in Fig. 3E, a pair of unambiguous redox peaks was recorded in the presence of SY, illustrating the existence of SY could improve the ability of electron transfer and reaction kinetics. Therefore, with the increase of SY concentration, the cathode ECL intensity was boosted. Besides, EIS also observed similar results, in the presence of SY made Rct smaller, compared with CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7, indicating that SY was supported to advance the electron transfer rate. Conversely, for the anode, SY UV–visible absorption spectrum at 480 nm partially overlaps the fluorescence excitation spectrum of BD-NCDs, and in the absence or presence of SY, the fluorescence lifetime change of BD-NCDs was negligible (3.48–3.85 ns) ( Fig. 5A and B). This undoubtedly explained that there was without energy transfer between BD-NCDs and SY, indicating the blue fluorescence of BD-NCDs could be effectively inhibited through the internal filtering effect (IFT). To acknowledge the mechanism that the luminescence of BD-NCDs was reduced, zeta potentials were recorded of BD-NCDs (−35.1 mV) and BD-NCDs+SY (−30 mV) (Fig. 5C), respectively. Both BD-NCDs and BD-NCDs+SY with the same charge further reacted that the decrease of ECL intensity of BD-NCDs was derived from the IFT. Therefore, the anode ECL signal decreased gradually with the increase in SY concentration (Fig. 2F). The above results undoubtedly confirmed that the constructed RECL sensor with effectively avoids false positive and false negative results, could detect SY sensitively and specifically, along with the decrease of anode intensity, the cathode intensity rises.

To evaluate the analytical performance of the RECL sensor, measure the response of SY with different concentrations to RECL under optimal conditions at − 1.5 V and + 0.75 V, respectively (Fig. S16). For anode and cathode, the ECL intensity possessed a merit linear relationship with SY concentration and was explained as in Fig. 5D, E. The cathode intensity (I
cathode

c = 13,630.9580 + 1.1068 CSY (R2 =0.9934) and I
a = 15,240.9033–2.3360 CSY (R2 =0.9965), respectively. The fine correlation between both ECL signals (single cathode and single anode) and SY concentration laid a foundation for RECL sensing. Therefore, to obtain the best performance and reliability, the RECL strategy was proposed to investigate the correlation of SY, which could effectively eliminate interference in a complex environment. Notably, I

Furthermore, according to the 3σ/b (S/N = 3), in which σ and b represented the standard deviation of the blank group and the slope of the corresponding standard curve, respectively, the limited detection (LOD) of LOD Ia/Ic , LOD Ia LOD Ic were also calculated as 0.005, 0.006 and 0.008 nM, respectively. The above results undoubtedly confirmed that the RECL had superior accuracy, stability, and ultra-low LOD (Fig. 5G and H). Compared with other methods for detecting SY (Table S2), the developed RECL sensor held a wider detection range and lower LOD and exhibited brilliant analytical performance.

In addition, to further evaluate the practicability and reliability of the RECL sensor. The content of SY in different foods was evaluated by adopting the spiked recovery strategy, including sports drinks (Fanta), fruit juice, and apple juice. As shown in Table S3, the recovery rate of SY in three different foods was 96.3%− 105.92%, and much satisfactory RSD was obtained. The above results further verified the potential application prospect and value of the RECL sensor in the real sample analysis and detection of SY.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Deep learning-assisted smartphone-based portable and intelligent analysis
ce:section.ce:para: In consideration of real-time portable detection, further explored the integration of RECL analysis into smartphones to achieve POCT of target molecules. The self-developed portable RECL smartphone sensor with integrated deep learning mainly consists of image acquisition and data processing. The screen-printing electrode was employed to substitute for the traditional three-electrode system. And smartphone completed luminous image acquisition and provided voltage, where the voltage was adjusted to 7 V through the adjustable circuit board ( Fig. 6A and C). The whole process was completed in the 3D printing device (Fig. 6B). Another important component was data processing, which was fulfilled through integrating deep learning with different algorithms (Yolov3, Yolov4, Yolov5s, Yolov5x, and efficientdet) into self-developed smartphone App (Fig. 6D). This effectively saves the time and cost of manual image processing and further promote the reliability and accuracy of train and identify the images. 1000 photos were trained to join different algorithms to statistically identify “useful” and “useless”. Subsequently, the self-developed color pattern analysis App was adopted to dissect the “useful” photos of the output results (Fig. 6E) and the relevant parameters (e. g. recall, precision, F1, AP, mAP, and lamr) of different algorithms were calculated. Comprehensively consider each parameter, and finally, Yolov3 with an accuracy of 99.19% was fixed as the algorithm for subsequent test items (Fig. S17 and Table S4).

According to self-reinforced RECL, the excellent sensitivity and specificity detection of SY, deep learning-assisted portable smartphone sensors has also been joined to verify their feasibility. The SY with different concentrations were added to the reaction system, the obtained luminous images were recorded (Fig. 6F and G), and the self-developed App was accepted to identify, calculate, and count red (R), green (G), blue (B), hue (H), saturation (S), value (V) and gray values (Fig. S18). Finally, the following multiple models: G/B-C, R/B-C, R/G-C, R-C, G-C, B-C, Gray-C S/V-C, H/V-C, H/S-C, H-C, S-C, V-C (where C represented the concentration of SY) were adopted to select the best R2 to establish a standard curve and automatically output on the smartphone screen. For the cathode, the B and R models exhibited well correlation with SY concentration, and the corresponding linear equations were Y= 0.00784 CSY+ 122.529 and Y= 0.01249 CSY+ 126.139, with a related coefficient (R2) R2 = 0.995 and R2 = 0.948, respectively (Fig. 6H-I). Similarly, R and Gray’s models displayed well correlation with SY concentration in the anode, and the corresponding linear equations were Y= −0.01035 CSY+ 182.282 (R2 =0.993) and Y= −0.009 CSY+ 170.856 (R2 =0.934), respectively (Fig. 6J-K). The accuracy and feasibility detection of SY based on a deep learning-assisted smartphone portable device was an attempt and exploration of combining RECL and artificial intelligence. Although relatively better-expected results had been achieved, which also provided valuable references for elaborately designing POCT, there were still great challenges in practical application.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The selectivity, stability, and reproducibility of the RECL sensor
ce:section.ce:para:
In addition, the specificity of the RECL sensor was further evaluated and explained in Fig. S19A, potential interfering substances were fixed at 100-fold the concentration of SY, including tartrazine, carmine, citric acid, rhodamine B, ascorbic acid, amaranth, glucose, nicotinamide, vitamin C and Na+, in the complex environment was selected to replace SY. The results exhibited that only SY had obvious changes, while other interfering substances possessed negligible changes to the RECL signal of the sensor, which sufficiently explained RECL possessed merit specificity. Similarly, RECL intensity was consistent, in the system where SY coexists with the above-interfering substances (Fig. S19B). This phenomenon also re-explained that RECL had superior selectivity for SY assayed. Moreover, other factors affecting sensor performance were also investigated, such as stability and reproducibility. The I
a
/I
c relative standard deviation (RSD) of the RECL strength of the electrode after 11 cycles of continuous scanning and the eight electrodes after the same treatment, as well as stored in the refrigerator at 4 °C for different days, were 0.61%, 1.02%, and 0.61%, respectively, reflecting the superior reproducibility and stability of the sensor (Figs. S9D and S19C-D). The above results comprehensively and systematically confirmed the superior selectivity, specificity, stability, and reproducibility of the sensor for appraising SY, which also laid the foundation for elaborately designing the POCT based on deep learning-assisted smartphone integration RECL.

In this work, CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 heterostructures sever as cathode luminophores with Zn/Cd-S-Sn bonds and enrich OVs were successfully synthesized based on the interface engineering strategy. Meanwhile, environment-friendly of BD-NCDs were utilized as anode luminophores, and a novel self-enhanced CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7/GCE+BD-NCDs+H2O2 RECL system was elaborately designed. Both experiment and DFT calculation systematically unfolded that CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 with heterointerface and enrich OVs had excellent dual functions for co-reactant H2O2. The formation of Zn/Cd-S-Sn bonds at the heterointerface could promote the transfer of electrons from CdZnS to Eu2Sn2O7 through bridging S, thus accelerating the electrochemical reaction kinetics to form a large number of •OH, significantly amplifying the ECL intensity of the cathode by 4.7 and 14.4 times respectively, compared to CdZnS and Eu2Sn2O7. However, when the H2O2 was adsorbed in the OVs, an oxygen-eating signal would appear, inducing the generation of O2 •-, which became an active intermediate to excite anode ECL. Additionally, DFT calculation also explained that both heterointerface and OVs held higher adsorption energy for H2O2 and lower energy barrier for •OH or O2 •- generation, which was helpful to realize the self-enhancement of CdZnS/Eu2Sn2O7 for RECL system. The excellent sensing performance of RECL was achieved with high sensitivity and specificity detection in the range of 0.1–4000 nM with a LOD was 0.005 nM. Most importantly, a convenient intelligence sensor system integrating deep learning with smartphones has been designed and successfully realized the sensitivity and on-site detection of SY. This provided a new method for the controllable synthesis of self-enhanced luminophores with heterointerfaces and OVs and also provided a novel idea for exploring the important role of heterointerfaces in the ECL luminescence mechanism. It also highlights the great potential of smartphone integration RECL in intelligent detection.
